summary:
The presence of facial and limb lesions in an infant strongly suggests a viral etiology, particularly herpetic infection due to characteristic skin manifestations associated with HSV. Topical antiviral treatment such as acyclovir supports this diagnosis since it targets herpes viruses effectively.  

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant information related to the lesion presentation and appropriate management based on known pathophysiological patterns.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent2’s reasoning strictly according to the requirements — just two sentences focusing solely on key clinical points regarding the cause of the infant's lesions.

Second sentence must tie back to diagnostic support via specific treatments mentioned ("topical antivirals like acyclovir") indicating herpes simplex virus as causal.

I avoid adding external knowledge not present in their original text; no mention was made about other organisms being considered so staying focused within stated facts ensures accuracy.

Final check confirms both elements align perfectly with core clinical clues from the case description without drifting into speculation.
